devanagari: श्रीकृष्णावतारोपक्रमः ब्रह्मकर्तृकं पृथिव्या आश्वासनं कंसस्य देवकीवधोद्योगाद् । वसुदेववचनेन निवृत्तिः षण्णां देवकीपुत्राणां कंसकतृको वधश...
english_devnagari: śrīkṛṣṇāvatāropakramaḥ brahmakartṛkaṃ pṛthivyā āśvāsanaṃ kaṃsasya devakīvadhodyogād vasudevavacanena nivṛttiḥ ṣaṇṇāṃ devakīputrāṇāṃ kaṃsakatṛko vadhaśca śrīrājovāca kathito vaṃśavistāro bhavatā somasūryayoḥ rājñāṃ ca ubhayavaṃśyānāṃ caritaṃ paramād‍bhutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The King (Parīkṣit) said:
Your worship has been pleased to narrate the detailed account of the Lunar and Solar dynasties and also the extremely wonderful exploits of kings, born in both these families.

devanagari: यदोश्च धर्मशीलस्य नितरां मुनिसत्तम । तत्रांशेन अवतीर्णस्य विष्णोर्वीर्याणि शंस नः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: yadośca dharmaśīlasya nitarāṃ munisattama tatrāṃśena avatīrṇasya viṣṇorvīryāṇi śaṃsa naḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb]

translation: (You have also described) especially the race of Yadu, of extremely pious nature. Oh the best of Sages! Do you now be pleased to describe to us the great deeds of Lord Viṣṇu who incarnated in his family, along with his part-manifestation (aṃśa viz. Balarāma).

devanagari: अवतीर्य यदोर्वंशे भगवान् भूतभावनः । कृतवान् यानि विश्वात्मा तानि नो वद विस्तरात् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: avatīrya yadorvaṃśe bhagavān bhūtabhāvanaḥ kṛtavān yāni viśvātmā tāni no vada vistarāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Please recount to us in details all those deeds that the Almighty Lord, the Maker (Protector) and the Soul of all beings, performed after incarnating in Yadu’s race.

devanagari: निवृत्ततर्षैः उपगीयमानाद् । भवौषधात् श्रोत्रमनोऽभिरामात् । क उत्तमश्लोकगुणानुवादात् । पुमान् विरज्येत विना पशुघ्नात् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: nivṛttatarṣaiḥ upagīyamānād bhavauṣadhāt śrotramano ka uttamaślokaguṇānuvādāt pumān virajyeta vinā paśughnāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: What man other than a slaughterer will desist from (listening to) the recital of the excellent attributes of Lord Kṛṣṇa, of auspicious glory—glory constantly sung by those who are freed from the influence of desires (and are liberated), a specific remedy for (release from) saṃsāra, and extremely pleasant to the ears and the mind.

devanagari: पितामहा मे समरेऽमरञ्जयैः । देवव्रताद्यातिरथैस्तिमिङ्गिलैः । दुरत्ययं कौरवसैन्यसागरं । कृत्वातरन् वत्सपदं स्म यत्प्लवाः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: pitāmahā me samare devavratādyātirathaistimiṅgilaiḥ duratyayaṃ kauravasainyasāgaraṃ kṛtvātaran vatsapadaṃ sma yatplavāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Using Him (Kṛṣṇa) as a raft, my grandfathers (i.e. grandfather Arjuna and granduncles such as Yudhiṣṭhira), crossed the ocean in the form of the Kaurava army, so difficult to tide over, due to its being infested with monstrous whales in the form of unrivalled (chariot-riding) warriors like Bhīṣma and others, the vanquishers of even celestials in battles, contemptuously treating it (the ocean-like army) like a (shallow) depression caused by a calf’s hoof.

devanagari: द्रौण्यस्त्रविप्लुष्टमिदं मदङ्गं । सन्तानबीजं कुरुपाण्डवानाम् । जुगोप कुक्षिं गत आत्तचक्रो । मातुश्च मे यः शरणं गतायाः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: drauṇyastravipluṣṭamidaṃ madaṅgaṃ santānabījaṃ kurupāṇḍavānām jugopa kukṣiṃ gata āttacakro mātuśca me yaḥ śaraṇaṃ gatāyāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: And armed with a discus (Sudarśana), He entered the womb of my mother (Uttarā) who sought his refuge and protected this body of mine—the seed for the continuity of the races of Kurus and Pāṇḍavas—while it (my body) was being scorched by the missile (Brahmāstra) discharged by Aśvatthāman, Droṇā’s son.

devanagari: वीर्याणि तस्याखिलदेहभाजां । अन्तर्बहिः पूरुषकालरूपैः । प्रयच्छतो मृत्युमुतामृतं च । मायामनुष्यस्य वदस्व विद्वन् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: vīryāṇi tasyākhiladehabhājāṃ antarbahiḥ pūruṣakālarūpaiḥ prayacchato mṛtyumutāmṛtaṃ ca māyāmanuṣyasya vadasva vidvan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ pre class 2 verb]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: Oh learned sage, be pleased to recount to me (for my benefit), the glorious exploits of him (Kṛṣṇa) who has assumed human form through his Māyā-power, and who pervades all embodied beings internally and externally, and who, as an Inner Controller and as a Destroyer (Time-Spirit)—severally dispenses Liberation to the spiritually-minded (“inner-seers”) and death, i.e. saṃsāra, to those with worldly propensities.

devanagari: रोहिण्यास्तनयः प्रोक्तो रामः संकर्षणस्त्वया । देवक्या गर्भसंबंधः कुतो देहान्तरं विना ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: rohiṇyāstanayaḥ prokto rāmaḥ saṃkarṣaṇastvayā devakyā garbhasaṃbaṃdhaḥ kuto dehāntaraṃ vinā
translation: Balarāma alias Saṅkarṣaṇa was (first) stated by you to be the son of Rohiṇī (vide supra 9.24.46). How could he be connected with the womb of Devakī (vide supra 9.24.54) without assuming another body?

devanagari: कस्मात् मुकुन्दो भगवान् पितुर्गेहाद् व्रजं गतः । क्व वासं ज्ञातिभिः सार्धं कृतवान् सात्वतां पतिः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: kasmāt mukundo bhagavān piturgehād vrajaṃ gataḥ kva vāsaṃ jñātibhiḥ sārdhaṃ kṛtavān sātvatāṃ patiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Why for did glorious Mukunda (Lord Kṛṣṇa) migrate from his father’s residence to Vraja? Where (in what places) did the Lord of Sātvatas (gods or a clan of Yadus) settle down with his kinsmen?

devanagari: व्रजे वसन् किं अकरोत् मधुपुर्यां च केशवः । भ्रातरं चावधीत् कंसं मातुः अद्धा अतदर्हणम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: vraje vasan kiṃ akarot madhupuryāṃ ca keśavaḥ bhrātaraṃ cāvadhīt kaṃsaṃ mātuḥ addhā atadarhaṇam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vas class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vas class 1 verb]

translation: What were the activities (lit. did he do) of Keśava while residing at Vraja, as well as in Mathurā, the city founded by demon Madhu? Why did he personally kill Kaṃsa, his mother’s brother who, as such, did not deserve it.

devanagari: देहं मानुषमाश्रित्य कति वर्षाणि वृष्णिभिः । यदुपुर्यां सहावात्सीत् पत्‍न्यः कत्यभवन् प्रभोः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: dehaṃ mānuṣamāśritya kati varṣāṇi vṛṣṇibhiḥ yadupuryāṃ sahāvātsīt pat‍nyaḥ katyabhavan prabhoḥ
translation: After assuming human body, for how many years did he stay at Dvārakā along with Vṛṣṇis (Yādavas)? How many were the consorts of the Lord?

devanagari: एतत् अन्यच्च सर्वं मे मुने कृष्णविचेष्टितम् । वक्तुमर्हसि सर्वज्ञ श्रद्दधानाय विस्तृतम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: etat anyacca sarvaṃ me mune kṛṣṇaviceṣṭitam vaktumarhasi sarvajña śraddadhānāya vistṛtam
translation: Oh Omniscient Sage! It behoves you to describe in details to me, a faithful devotee, this and every other deed performed by Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: नैषातिदुःसहा क्षुन्मां त्यक्तोदं अपि बाधते । पिबन्तं त्वन्मुखाम्भोज अच्युतं हरिकथामृतम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: naiṣātiduḥsahā kṣunmāṃ tyaktodaṃ api bādhate pibantaṃ tvanmukhāmbhoja acyutaṃ harikathāmṛtam
translation: Though I have given up even drinking of water in this (life-long) fast, this extremely unbearable hunger does not torment me, as I drink the nectar of Hari’s stories flowing from the moon-lotus in the form of your mouth.

devanagari: सूत उवाच । एवं निशम्य भृगुनन्दन साधुवादं । वैयासकिः स भगवान् अथ विष्णुरातम् । प्रत्यर्च्य कृष्णचरितं कलिकल्मषघ्नं । व्याहर्तुमारभत भागव...
english_devnagari: sūta uvāca evaṃ niśamya bhṛgunandana sādhuvādaṃ vaiyāsakiḥ sa bhagavān atha viṣṇurātam pratyarcya kṛṣṇacaritaṃ kalikalmaṣaghnaṃ vyāhartumārabhata bhāgavatapradhānaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Sūta said:
Oh Śaunaka, having listened to the laudable inquiry, the worshipful son of Vyāsa (Śuka) who was the foremost among the votaries of viṣṇu, complimented king Parīkṣit (one protected by Viṣṇu) and commenced to extol the life and episodes of Lord Kṛṣṇa, which destroy the sins and impurities of Kali (Or which remove the miseries of saṃsāra.)

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । सम्यग्व्यवसिता बुद्धिः तव राजर्षिसत्तम । वासुदेवकथायां ते यज्जाता नैष्ठिकी रतिः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca samyagvyavasitā buddhiḥ tava rājarṣisattama vāsudevakathāyāṃ te yajjātā naiṣṭhikī ratiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Oh foremost among saintly kings! Your mind has been properly directed, inasmuch as the utmost and firmest devotion to the stories of Vāsudeva has been engendered in you.

devanagari: वासुदेवकथाप्रश्नः पुरुषान् त्रीन् पुनाति हि । वक्तारं पृच्छकं श्रोतॄन् तत्पादसलिलं यथा ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: vāsudevakathāpraśnaḥ puruṣān trīn punāti hi vaktāraṃ pṛcchakaṃ śrotṝn tatpādasalilaṃ yathā
translation: Inquiries about the stories of Vāsudeva certainly purify all the three persons concerned, namely, the reciter, the inquirer and the members of the audience, just as water washing his feet i.e. the Gaṅgā (Or the water washing Śālagrāma) purifies all.

devanagari: भूमिः दृप्तनृपव्याज दैत्यानीकशतायुतैः । आक्रान्ता भूरिभारेण ब्रह्माणं शरणं ययौ ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūmiḥ dṛptanṛpavyāja daityānīkaśatāyutaiḥ ākrāntā bhūribhāreṇa brahmāṇaṃ śaraṇaṃ yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Mother Earth which was overburdened with the crushing weight of millions and millions of the hosts of Daityas who assumed the guise of arrogant kings, sought refuge to god Brahmā.

devanagari: गौर्भूत्वा अश्रुमुखी खिन्ना क्रन्दन्ती करुणं विभोः । उपस्थितान्तिके तस्मै व्यसनं स्वं अवोचत ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: gaurbhūtvā aśrumukhī khinnā krandantī karuṇaṃ vibhoḥ upasthitāntike tasmai vyasanaṃ svaṃ avocata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ khid class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ khid class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ khid class 7 verb]
[nominative dual from √ krand class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ krand class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ krand class 1 verb]

translation: Taking the form of a depressed and piteously lowing cow and with her face overflowing with tears, she approached god Brahmā’s presence and represented to him her grievous plight.

devanagari: ब्रह्मा तद् उपधार्याथ सह देवैस्तया सह । जगाम सत्रिनयनः तीरं क्षीरपयोनिधेः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmā tad upadhāryātha saha devaistayā saha jagāma satrinayanaḥ tīraṃ kṣīrapayonidheḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Realizing the piteous condition of the earth, god Brahmā accompanied by the three-eyed god Śiva and other gods, came to the shore of the milky ocean, along with her.

devanagari: तत्र गत्वा जगन्नाथं देवदेवं वृषाकपिम् । पुरुषं पुरुषसूक्तेन उपतस्थे समाहितः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tatra gatvā jagannāthaṃ devadevaṃ vṛṣākapim puruṣaṃ puruṣasūktena upatasthe samāhitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: After reaching there, he with perfect concentration of mind, praised in terms of the Puruṣa-Śūkta the Supreme Person, the Lord of the world, the god of gods, the absolutely blissful Enjoyer of sacrifices.

devanagari: गिरं समाधौ गगने समीरितां । निशम्य वेधास्त्रिदशानुवाच ह । गां पौरुषीं मे श्रृणुतामराः पुनः । विधीयतां आशु तथैव मा चिरम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: giraṃ samādhau gagane samīritāṃ niśamya vedhāstridaśānuvāca ha gāṃ pauruṣīṃ me śrṛṇutāmarāḥ punaḥ vidhīyatāṃ āśu tathaiva mā ciram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Listening to the voice uttered in the sky while he was meditating (upon the Lord), god Brahmā spoke out to the celestials, “Oh immortals, hear from me the words of the Supreme Person and comply immediately with them. Let there be no delay.”

devanagari: पुरैव पुंसा अवधृतो धराज्वरो । भवद्भिः अंशैः यदुषूपजन्यताम् । स यावद् उर्व्या भरं इश्वरेश्वरः । स्वकालशक्त्या क्षपयन् चरेद् भुवि ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: puraiva puṃsā avadhṛto dharājvaro bhavadbhiḥ aṃśaiḥ yaduṣūpajanyatām sa yāvad urvyā bharaṃ iśvareśvaraḥ svakālaśaktyā kṣapayan cared bhuvi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ kṣap class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ kṣap class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ kṣī ], [vocative single from √ kṣī ]

translation: The affliction of Mother Earth was already understood by the Supreme Person before we appealed to him. You should now take birth in the Yadu Clan, in your partmanifestation, while the Lord of gods will walk over the earth, reducing its burden through his potency called Time.

devanagari: वसुदेवगृहे साक्षाद् भगवान् पुरुषः परः । जनिष्यते तत्प्रियार्थं संभवन्तु सुरस्त्रियः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: vasudevagṛhe sākṣād bhagavān puruṣaḥ paraḥ janiṣyate tatpriyārthaṃ saṃbhavantu surastriyaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [dative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [dative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [dative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [dative single from √ jan class 4 verb]
[dative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [dative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [dative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [dative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation:
The glorious Supreme Lord will directly manifest Himself in the house of Vasudeva. Let the heavenly damsels be born (on the earth) to minister to his pleasures.
[Bhāgavata Candrikā’s text adds:]
23(A). As commanded by him, let the sages be made to assume the form of cattle for the purpose of propitiating Lord Viṣṇu by yielding him milk, Oh gods.

devanagari: वासुदेवकलानन्तः सहस्रवदनः स्वराट् । अग्रतो भविता देवो हरेः प्रियचिकीर्षया ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: vāsudevakalānantaḥ sahasravadanaḥ svarāṭ agrato bhavitā devo hareḥ priyacikīrṣayā
translation: The self-effulgent thousand-faced Lord Śeṣa imbued with a part ofVāsudeva, will be born as the elder brother, with a view to catering to his pleasures.

devanagari: विष्णोर्माया भगवती यया सम्मोहितं जगत् । आदिष्टा प्रभुणांशेन कार्यार्थे संभविष्यति ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣṇormāyā bhagavatī yayā sammohitaṃ jagat ādiṣṭā prabhuṇāṃśena kāryārthe saṃbhaviṣyati
translation: The divine Yoga-Māyā of Viṣṇu who has deluded the world, has been commanded to take birth in part-manifestation for fulfilling his mission.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्यादिश्यामरगणान् प्रजापतिपतिः विभुः । आश्वास्य च महीं गीर्भिः स्वधाम परमं ययौ ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityādiśyāmaragaṇān prajāpatipatiḥ vibhuḥ āśvāsya ca mahīṃ gīrbhiḥ svadhāma paramaṃ yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having directed the hosts of gods in this way, and consoling the Earth in reassuring words, god Brahrnā, the Ruler of Prajāpatis, repaired to his exalted region.

devanagari: शूरसेनो यदुपतिः मथुरां आवसन् पुरीम् । माथुरान् शूरसेनांश्च विषयान् बुभुजे पुरा ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śūraseno yadupatiḥ mathurāṃ āvasan purīm māthurān śūrasenāṃśca viṣayān bubhuje purā

translation: In former times, Śūrasena, the chief of the Yadu clan, ruled over the territories of Mathurā and Śūrasena, while residing in the city of Mathurā.

devanagari: राजधानी ततः साभूत् सर्वयादव भूभुजाम् । मथुरा भगवान् यत्र नित्यं सन्निहितो हरिः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: rājadhānī tataḥ sābhūt sarvayādava bhūbhujām mathurā bhagavān yatra nityaṃ sannihito hariḥ
translation: Thenceforth, Mathurā became the metropolis of all kings of the Yādava dynasty. In that city, the glorious Lord Hari ever exhibits his presence in a special way.

devanagari: तस्यां तु कर्हिचित् शौरिः वसुदेवः कृतोद्वहः । देवक्या सूर्यया सार्धं प्रयाणे रथमारुहत् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ tu karhicit śauriḥ vasudevaḥ kṛtodvahaḥ devakyā sūryayā sārdhaṃ prayāṇe rathamāruhat

translation: In that city, on one occasion, Vasudeva,son of Śūra, whose marriage with Devakī was celebrated, ascended the chariot with his newly wedded wife, to proceed to his place after marriage.

devanagari: उग्रसेनसुतः कंसः स्वसुः प्रियचिकीर्षया । रश्मीन् हयानां जग्राह रौक्मै रथशतैर्वृतः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: ugrasenasutaḥ kaṃsaḥ svasuḥ priyacikīrṣayā raśmīn hayānāṃ jagrāha raukmai rathaśatairvṛtaḥ

[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: Kaṃsa, the son of Ugrasena, being desirous of pleasing his sister (cousin), took over the reins of the horses, surrounded by hundreds of gold-plated chariots.

devanagari: चतुःशतं पारिबर्हं गजानां हेममालिनाम् । अश्वानां अयुतं सार्धं रथानां च त्रिषट्शतम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: catuḥśataṃ pāribarhaṃ gajānāṃ hemamālinām aśvānāṃ ayutaṃ sārdhaṃ rathānāṃ ca triṣaṭśatam
translation: At the time of departure, Devaka who was fond of his daughter, gave, as wedding presents, four hundred elephants caparisoned with gold chains, fifteen thousand horses, eighteen hundred chariots and two hundred comely maidservants, adorned with gold ornaments.

devanagari: दासीनां सुकुमारीणां द्वे शते समलंकृते । दुहित्रे देवकः प्रादात् याने दुहितृवत्सलः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: dāsīnāṃ sukumārīṇāṃ dve śate samalaṃkṛte duhitre devakaḥ prādāt yāne duhitṛvatsalaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]

translation: At the time of departure, Devaka who was fond of his daughter, gave, as wedding presents, four hundred elephants caparisoned with gold chains, fifteen thousand horses, eighteen hundred chariots and two hundred comely maidservants, adorned with gold ornaments.

devanagari: शंखतूर्यमृदंगाश्च नेदुः दुन्दुभयः समम् । प्रयाणप्रक्रमे तावत् वरवध्वोः सुमंगलम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: śaṃkhatūryamṛdaṃgāśca neduḥ dundubhayaḥ samam prayāṇaprakrame tāvat varavadhvoḥ sumaṃgalam
translation: As the procession of the bride and the bridegroom started, conchs, clarionets, drums and kettle-drums were sounded simultaneously in the most auspicious manner.

devanagari: पथि प्रग्रहिणं कंसं आभाष्य आह अशरीरवाक् । अस्यास्त्वां अष्टमो गर्भो हन्ता यां वहसे अबुध ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: pathi pragrahiṇaṃ kaṃsaṃ ābhāṣya āha aśarīravāk asyāstvāṃ aṣṭamo garbho hantā yāṃ vahase abudha
translation: On the way, an incorporeal voice from the sky addressed Kaṃsa who was holding the reins: “Oh foolish fellow, the eighth son from the womb of her whom you are conducting, shall slay you”.

devanagari: इत्युक्तः स खलः पापो भोजानां कुलपांसनः । भगिनीं हन्तुमारब्धः खड्गपाणिः कचेऽग्रहीत् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktaḥ sa khalaḥ pāpo bhojānāṃ kulapāṃsanaḥ bhaginīṃ hantumārabdhaḥ khaḍgapāṇiḥ kace

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: Being thus warned, the wicked sinful fellow, who was a disgrace to the Bhoja family, with a sword in hand, seized his sister by her braids of hair and proceeded to kill her.

devanagari: तं जुगुप्सितकर्माणं नृशंसं निरपत्रपम् । वसुदेवो महाभाग उवाच परिसान्त्वयन् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ jugupsitakarmāṇaṃ nṛśaṃsaṃ nirapatrapam vasudevo mahābhāga uvāca parisāntvayan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The great-souled Vasudeva pleaded in conciliatory terms with the cruel, shameless perpetrator of disgraceful deeds.

devanagari: श्रीवसुदेव उवाच । श्लाघनीयगुणः शूरैः भवान् भोजयशस्करः । स कथं भगिनीं हन्यात् स्त्रियं उद्वाहपर्वणि ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvasudeva uvāca ślāghanīyaguṇaḥ śūraiḥ bhavān bhojayaśaskaraḥ sa kathaṃ bhaginīṃ hanyāt striyaṃ udvāhaparvaṇi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Vasudeva urged:
You possess virtues extolled by the brave and are the glory to the Bhoja family. As such how is it that you (attempt to) kill your sister on the festive occasion of her marriage.

devanagari: मृत्युर्जन्मवतां वीर देहेन सह जायते । अद्य वाब्दशतान्ते वा मृत्युर्वै प्राणिनां ध्रुवः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: mṛtyurjanmavatāṃ vīra dehena saha jāyate adya vābdaśatānte vā mṛtyurvai prāṇināṃ dhruvaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: (If you say that you are doing so as a precautionary measure), death is born along with the body of all creatures, Oh warrior! Death is inevitable to all creatures, whether it may be today or at the end of hundred years.

devanagari: देहे पञ्चत्वमापन्ने देही कर्मानुगोऽवशः । देहान्तरं अनुप्राप्य प्राक्तनं त्यजते वपुः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: dehe pañcatvamāpanne dehī karmānugo dehāntaraṃ anuprāpya prāktanaṃ tyajate vapuḥ
translation: When the body is on the point of dissolving in five component-Elements (i.e. at the time of death), the Soul residing in it automatically, under the force of his karmas (as good as) obtains another body and quits his previous frame.

devanagari: व्रजन् तिष्ठन् पदैकेन यथैवैकेन गच्छति । यथा तृणजलूकैवं देही कर्मगतिं गतः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: vrajan tiṣṭhan padaikena yathaivaikena gacchati yathā tṛṇajalūkaivaṃ dehī karmagatiṃ gataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vraj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vraj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]
[imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Just as a man while walking, stands with his one foot established on the earth, while the other paces forward, or just as a caterpillar leaves the (previous) blade of grass only when it catches the hold of another, similarly does an embodied being, caught in the current of karmas.

devanagari: स्वप्ने यथा पश्यति देहमीदृशं । मनोरथेन अभिनिविष्टचेतनः । दृष्टश्रुताभ्यां मनसानुचिन्तयन् । प्रपद्यते तत् किमपि ह्यपस्मृतिः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: svapne yathā paśyati dehamīdṛśaṃ manorathena abhiniviṣṭacetanaḥ dṛṣṭaśrutābhyāṃ manasānucintayan prapadyate tat kimapi hyapasmṛtiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Just as a person whose mind is obsessed with a strong desire, and who is mentally brooding over the objects of his desires, with his mind deeply impressed by what he has seen or heard, seems to realize it in dreams, and identifies himself with it, for getting himself (his body in the waking state). So does the soul attain his new body quitting the old one.

devanagari: यतो यतो धावति दैवचोदितं । मनो विकारात्मकमाप पञ्चसु । गुणेषु मायारचितेषु देह्यसौ । प्रपद्यमानः सह तेन जायते ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: yato yato dhāvati daivacoditaṃ mano vikārātmakamāpa pañcasu guṇeṣu māyāraciteṣu dehyasau prapadyamānaḥ saha tena jāyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ cud ], [accusative single from √ cud ]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
To whatever modification of five bhūtas created by Māyā that the mind consisting of various emotions and passions feels impelled by destiny, and flows to it, and is attached at the time of death, the soul abiding in the body identifies itself with it and passes into that form along with the mind.
42a. Being impelled by Destiny, the mind, consisting of emotions and passions, feels impelled to, flows to and is attached to (at the time of death) whatever modifications of the five Bhūtas created by Māyā, the soul abiding in the body identifies itself with that form, and passes into it along with the mind.

devanagari: ज्योतिर्यथैव उदकपार्थिवेष्वदः । समीरवेगानुगतं विभाव्यते । एवं स्वमायारचितेष्वसौ पुमान् । गुणेषु रागानुगतो विमुह्यति ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: jyotiryathaiva udakapārthiveṣvadaḥ samīravegānugataṃ vibhāvyate evaṃ svamāyāraciteṣvasau pumān guṇeṣu rāgānugato vimuhyati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: Just as a luminous body (e.g. the Sun or the Moon) which, when reflected in water in an earthen container, appears to move or change according to the speed of the wind that ruffles the liquid reflecting medium (though actually—the original luminary—does not shake), so does the embodied soul, through attachment, enter the bodies or forms created by his own Māyā and is overcome with delusion.

devanagari: तस्मात् न कस्यचिद् द्रोहं आचरेत् स तथाविधः । आत्मनः क्षेममन्विच्छन् द्रोग्धुर्वै परतो भयम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāt na kasyacid drohaṃ ācaret sa tathāvidhaḥ ātmanaḥ kṣemamanvicchan drogdhurvai parato bhayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Therefore, a person being so conditioned but seeking his own welfare, should not do wrong to anyone. For there is fear to the wrong-doers from others (such as god of death, the person wronged etc.).

devanagari: एषा तव अनुजा बाला कृपणा पुत्रिकोपमा । हन्तुं नार्हसि कल्याणीं इमां त्वं दीनवत्सलः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣā tava anujā bālā kṛpaṇā putrikopamā hantuṃ nārhasi kalyāṇīṃ imāṃ tvaṃ dīnavatsalaḥ
translation: This poor girl, your younger sister, is just like a daughter to you (or a lifeless doll). It does not become you who are kind to the helpless, to kill her who has just completed the marriage rites.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं स सामभिर्भेदैः बोध्यमानोऽपि दारुणः । न न्यवर्तत कौरव्य पुरुषादान् अनुव्रतः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ sa sāmabhirbhedaiḥ bodhyamāno na nyavartata kauravya puruṣādān anuvrataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
Oh descendant of Kuru! Even though admonished with conciliatory words and words inspiring fear (of dire consequences), the relentless Kaṃsa, following the ways of demons, did not desist (from his determination).

devanagari: निर्बन्धं तस्य तं ज्ञात्वा विचिन्त्यानकदुन्दुभिः । प्राप्तं कालं प्रतिव्योढुं इदं तत्रान्वपद्यत ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: nirbandhaṃ tasya taṃ jñātvā vicintyānakadundubhiḥ prāptaṃ kālaṃ prativyoḍhuṃ idaṃ tatrānvapadyata
translation: Observing his firm determination (to kill Devakī), Vasudeva thought for a while how to avert this imminent danger for the time being, and devised the following expedient for it.

devanagari: मृत्युर्बुद्धिमतापोह्यो यावद्‍बुद्धिबलोदयम् । यद्यसौ न निवर्तेत नापराधोऽस्ति देहिनः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: mṛtyurbuddhimatāpohyo yāvad‍buddhibalodayam yadyasau na nivarteta nāparādho
translation: (He thought to himself:) “An intelligent person should try to avert death to the utmost limit of his intellectual and physical abilities. So if, despite these, it could not be warded off, no blame will be attached to him.

devanagari: प्रदाय मृत्यवे पुत्रान् मोचये कृपणां इमाम् । सुता मे यदि जायेरन् मृत्युर्वा न म्रियेत चेत् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: pradāya mṛtyave putrān mocaye kṛpaṇāṃ imām sutā me yadi jāyeran mṛtyurvā na mriyeta cet

[optative active second plural], [optative middle third single], [optative passive third single]

translation: I shall rescue this helpless girl by promising in exchange handing over of her sons to Death (viz. Kaṃsa who is going to kill them), provided the sons are born or he (Kaṃsa) does not die in the meanwhile.

devanagari: विपर्ययो वा किं न स्याद् गतिर्धातुः१ दुरत्यया । उपस्थितो निवर्तेत निवृत्तः पुनरापतेत् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: viparyayo vā kiṃ na syād gatirdhātuḥ1 duratyayā upasthito nivarteta nivṛttaḥ punarāpatet
translation: Or might not the contrary take place (i.e. my son might kill him), unscrutable are the ways of the Providence; for the imminent may be averted and what is averted may happen again.

devanagari: अग्नेर्यथा दारुवियोगयोगयोः । अदृष्टतोऽन्यन्न निमित्तमस्ति । एवं हि जन्तोरपि दुर्विभाव्यः । शरीर संयोगवियोगहेतुः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: agneryathā dāruviyogayogayoḥ adṛṣṭato evaṃ hi jantorapi durvibhāvyaḥ śarīra saṃyogaviyogahetuḥ
translation: Just as, in a forest conflagrations, there is no apparent cause (except Fate) for its coming in contact with a particular tree or remaining aloof from another, so also in the case of a created being, the cause of its being—associated or dissociated with a particular body is very difficult to be comprehended.”

devanagari: एवं विमृश्य तं पापं यावद् आत्मनिदर्शनम् । पूजयामास वै शौरिः बहुमानपुरःसरम् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vimṛśya taṃ pāpaṃ yāvad ātmanidarśanam pūjayāmāsa vai śauriḥ bahumānapuraḥsaram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Having carefully considered (this problem) to the best of his intellectual capacity, Vasudeva respectfully addressed the wicked fellow, with (apparently) great esteem.

devanagari: प्रसन्न वदनाम्भोजो नृशंसं निरपत्रपम् । मनसा दूयमानेन विहसन् इदमब्रवीत् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: prasanna vadanāmbhojo nṛśaṃsaṃ nirapatrapam manasā dūyamānena vihasan idamabravīt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ man class 4 verb], [instrumental single from √ man class 8 verb]
[instrumental single from √ man class 4 verb], [instrumental single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Simulating a blooming lotus-like (cheerful) countenance, in spite of his heart full of pain, he smilingly spoke to that hard-hearted and shameless fellow.

devanagari: श्रीवसुदेव उवाच । न ह्यस्यास्ते भयं सौम्य यद् वाक् आहाशरीरिणी । पुत्रान् समर्पयिष्येऽस्या यतस्ते भयमुत्थितम् ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvasudeva uvāca na hyasyāste bhayaṃ saumya yad vāk āhāśarīriṇī putrān samarpayiṣye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
Vasudeva said:
Oh gentle-natured Kaṃsa! There is certainly no fear to you from this girl as has been intimated to you by the incorporeal voice from the sky. I shall handover to you her sons from whom you apprehend danger.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । स्वसुर्वधात् निववृते कंसः तद्वाक्यसारवित् । वसुदेवोऽपि तं प्रीतः प्रशस्य प्राविशद् गृहम् ॥ ५५ ॥
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Kaṃsa, who properly understood the significance of Vasudeva’s words, desisted from killing his sister (Devakī). Even Vasudeva who was pleased, extolled him and entered his palace.

devanagari: अथ काल उपावृत्ते देवकी सर्वदेवता । पुत्रान् प्रसुषुवे चाष्टौ कन्यां चैवानुवत्सरम् ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: atha kāla upāvṛtte devakī sarvadevatā putrān prasuṣuve cāṣṭau kanyāṃ caivānuvatsaram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ u class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ u class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ u class 2 verb]

translation: After a lapse of time, in due course, Devakī who was devoted to the Lord who is the soul of all deities and was permeated with the divinities, gave birth to eight sons and a daughter, one every year.

devanagari: कीर्तिमन्तं प्रथमजं कंसायानकदुन्दुभिः । अर्पयामास कृच्छ्रेण सोऽनृताद् अतिविह्वलः ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: kīrtimantaṃ prathamajaṃ kaṃsāyānakadundubhiḥ arpayāmāsa kṛcchreṇa so
translation: Vasudeva who (by nature) was intensely agitated to act falsely, handed over with great pains his first born son, Kīrtimān, to Kaṃsa.

devanagari: किं दुःसहं नु साधूनां विदुषां किं अपेक्षितम् । किं अकार्यं कदर्याणां दुस्त्यजं किं धृतात्मनाम् ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ duḥsahaṃ nu sādhūnāṃ viduṣāṃ kiṃ apekṣitam kiṃ akāryaṃ kadaryāṇāṃ dustyajaṃ kiṃ dhṛtātmanām
translation: What is there which a truthful person (like Vasudeva) cannot endure? Is there anything which the knowers of the truth (that the Lord alone is the reality) long for? (Did Vasudeva think that Kaṃsa would spare his child when handed over personally? No.). There is nothing which the wicked would not do. (How is it that Devakī parted with the child?) What thing would they not sacrifice who cherish Hari in their hearts?

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा समत्वं तत् शौरेः सत्ये चैव व्यवस्थितिम् । कंसस्तुष्टमना राजन् प्रहसन् इदमब्रवीत् ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā samatvaṃ tat śaureḥ satye caiva vyavasthitim kaṃsastuṣṭamanā rājan prahasan idamabravīt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tuṣ class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ tuṣ class 4 verb]

[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Seeing the equanimity and firmness in truth of Vasudeva Kaṃsa was highly pleased with him and spoke to him smilingly: Oh King.

devanagari: प्रतियातु कुमारोऽयं न ह्यस्मादस्ति मे भयम् । अष्टमाद् युवयोर्गर्भान् मृत्युर्मे विहितः किल ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: pratiyātu kumāro aṣṭamād yuvayorgarbhān mṛtyurme vihitaḥ kila
translation: “Let this boy return (to his mother) as there is no danger from him to me, as my death has been ordained from your eighth child.”

devanagari: तथेति सुतमादाय ययौ आनकदुन्दुभिः । नाभ्यनन्दत तद्वाक्यं असतोऽविजितात्मनः ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: tatheti sutamādāya yayau ānakadundubhiḥ nābhyanandata tadvākyaṃ asato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Taking back his son in compliance of Kaṃsa’s words, Vasudeva returned home. He was however not happy over the words of that wicked Kaṃsa of uncontrolled mind.
[Additional verse in Padaratnāvalī’s text:]
61(A). Then the divine sage Nārada, the son of Brahmā, called upon Kaṃsa, and conferring with him privately spoke as follows.

devanagari: नन्दाद्या ये व्रजे गोपा याश्चामीषां च योषितः । वृष्णयो वसुदेवाद्या देवक्याद्या यदुस्त्रियः ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: nandādyā ye vraje gopā yāścāmīṣāṃ ca yoṣitaḥ vṛṣṇayo vasudevādyā devakyādyā yadustriyaḥ
translation: “Whatever cowherds of whom Nanda is the chief, there are in Vraja and again all their women and all Vṛṣṇis headed by Vasudeva and Yādava women of whom Devakī is the foremost—all the kinsmen, relations and friends of both Nanda and Vasudeva who are loyal to Kaṃsa—are mostly gods.: (while) those who are the followers of Kaṃsa.

devanagari: सर्वे वै देवताप्राया उभयोरपि भारत । ज्ञातयो बन्धुसुहृदो ये च कंसं अनुव्रताः ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: sarve vai devatāprāyā ubhayorapi bhārata jñātayo bandhusuhṛdo ye ca kaṃsaṃ anuvratāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation:
“Whatever cowherds of whom Nanda is the chief, there are in Vraja and again all their women and all Vṛṣṇis headed by Vasudeva and Yādava women of whom Devakī is the foremost—all the kinsmen, relations and friends of both Nanda and Vasudeva who are loyal to Kaṃsa—are mostly gods.: (while) those who are the followers of Kaṃsa.
[Bhāgavata Candrikā’s text adds:]
63(A). Are verily all asuras who trouble the world.”

devanagari: एतत् कंसाय भगवान् शशंसाभ्येत्य नारदः । भूमेर्भारायमाणानां दैत्यानां च वधोद्यमम् ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: etat kaṃsāya bhagavān śaśaṃsābhyetya nāradaḥ bhūmerbhārāyamāṇānāṃ daityānāṃ ca vadhodyamam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: (Considering that the Lord would not incarnate earlier by Kaṃsa’s such peaceful attitude) Nārada approached Kaṃsa and advised him as above, apprising him of the endeavours made (by god Brahmā and others) for the annihilation of Daityas who became a burden to the earth.

devanagari: ऋषेः विनिर्गमे कंसो यदून् मत्वा सुरान् इति । देवक्या गर्भसंभूतं विष्णुं च स्ववधं प्रति ॥ ६५ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣeḥ vinirgame kaṃso yadūn matvā surān iti devakyā garbhasaṃbhūtaṃ viṣṇuṃ ca svavadhaṃ prati
translation: On the departure of the sage, Kaṃsa looked upon all Yadus as gods and every child born of Devakī as Viṣṇu intent upon slaying himself.

devanagari: देवकीं वसुदेवं च निगृह्य निगडैर्गृहे । जातं जातं अहन् पुत्रं तयोः अजनशंकया ॥ ६६ ॥
english_devnagari: devakīṃ vasudevaṃ ca nigṛhya nigaḍairgṛhe jātaṃ jātaṃ ahan putraṃ tayoḥ ajanaśaṃkayā

translation: Fettering Devakī and Vasudeva in chains, he imprisoned them in their house and killed every new-born child, fearing him to be god Viṣṇu.

devanagari: मातरं पितरं भ्रातॄन् सर्वांश्च सुहृदस्तथा । घ्नन्ति ह्यसुतृपो लुब्धा राजानः प्रायशो भुवि ॥ ६७ ॥
english_devnagari: mātaraṃ pitaraṃ bhrātṝn sarvāṃśca suhṛdastathā ghnanti hyasutṛpo lubdhā rājānaḥ prāyaśo bhuvi

translation: Generally, the kings on the earth who are covetous and thirsty after the lives of others, kill their mother, father, brothers and all friends as well.

devanagari: आत्मानं इह सञ्जातं जानन् प्राग् विष्णुना हतम् । महासुरं कालनेमिं यदुभिः स व्यरुध्यत ॥ ६८ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmānaṃ iha sañjātaṃ jānan prāg viṣṇunā hatam mahāsuraṃ kālanemiṃ yadubhiḥ sa vyarudhyata

[nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Realising himself to be the great asura Kālanemi whom Viṣṇu had formerly killed, born here again, he commenced hostilities with Yādavas.

devanagari: उग्रसेनं च पितरं यदुभोजान्धकाधिपम् । स्वयं निगृह्य बुभुजे शूरसेनान् महाबलः ॥ ६९ ॥
english_devnagari: ugrasenaṃ ca pitaraṃ yadubhojāndhakādhipam svayaṃ nigṛhya bubhuje śūrasenān mahābalaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Being endowed with enormous power, he arrested his father Ugrasena, the king of Yādavas, Bhojas and Andhakas and took over the government of the Śūrasena region.
